judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports fully satisfy all four expectations with correct git reasoning; comparable, with Report 2 adding a merge-conflict check as a slight extra.

report_1.evidence: **1. Base branch: `main`.** The dependency branch `feat/weighted-scorer` no longer needs to be the base because its content already landed on `main`

report_2.evidence: **1. Base branch: `main`.** Every PR should target the trunk. The only other candidate base is `feat/weighted-scorer` (the branch this work was stacked on), but that branch is already dead as a base

report_1.evidence: `feat/pairwise-metric` was created from the tip of `feat/weighted-scorer` ... That starting point has been **squash-merged into main** as `1971f08` (the "(#42)" PR-number suffix and the empty content diff confirm it), so the W1–W3 commits are content-wise on main but not ancestry-wise

report_2.evidence: `feat/pairwise-metric` was created from the tip of `feat/weighted-scorer` ... That starting point has since been **squash-merged into `main`**: on June 13, `1971f08 (#42)` landed on `main` ... the *content* is in `main` but the W1–W3 *commits* are not ancestors of `main`

report_1.evidence: So no, not all of it is pairwise work. ... `scoring/scorer.py`: full rewrite to weighted scoring ... entirely weighted-scorer work (W1/W2), **not** pairwise work. ... GitHub computes PR diffs from the merge-base, which is still `65204ad`, so the W changes reappear.

report_2.evidence: **Not all of it is pairwise work.** ... The rest — all of `scoring/scorer.py` and most of `metrics.py` — is the weighted-scorer work (W1–W3). It appears in the diff because the branch was cut from `feat/weighted-scorer` ... the merge base with `main` is still `65204ad`, so git counts W1–W3 as "this branch's changes"

report_1.evidence: Rebase the branch onto main, dropping the already-merged parent commits: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`. ... Verify: `git log --oneline main..feat/pairwise-metric` shows exactly 2 commits, and `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric` now matches the 5-line two-dot diff

report_2.evidence: `git rebase --onto origin/main ac9d505 feat/pairwise-metric` (replays exactly X1 ... and X2 ...). ... Verify the result: `git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline` shows only the two X commits; `git diff origin/main` shows only `prompts/grader.txt` (+1) and `scoring/metrics.py` (+4)
